segments.text: At that time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the grain fields. His disciples were hungry and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
But the Pharisees, when they saw it, said to him,
Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: But he said to them,
Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, and those who were with him:
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: how he entered into God’s house and ate the show bread, which was not lawful for him to eat, nor for those who were with him, but only for the priests?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: Or have you not read in the law that on the Sabbath day the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath and are guiltless?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: But I tell you that one greater than the temple is here.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’you wouldn’t have condemned the guiltless.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: He departed from there and went into their synagogue.
And behold, there was a man with a withered hand. They asked him,
Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath day?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: so that they might accuse him.
He said to them,
What man is there among you who has one sheep, and if this one falls into a pit on the Sabbath day, won’t he grab on to it and lift it out?
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath day.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: Then he told the man,
Stretch out your hand.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: He stretched it out; and it was restored whole, just like the other.
But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how they might destroy him.
Jesus, perceiving that, withdrew from there. Great multitudes followed him; and he healed them all,
and commanded them that they should not make him known,
that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,
Behold, my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved in whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit on him. He will proclaim justice to the nations.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: He will not strive, nor shout, neither will anyone hear his voice in the streets.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: He won’t break a bruised reed. He won’t quench a smoking flax, until he leads justice to victory.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: In his name, the nations will hope.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees, when they saw it,

segments.text: Then one possessed by a demon, blind and mute, was brought to him; and he healed him, so that the blind and mute man both spoke and saw.
All the multitudes were amazed, and said,
Can this be the son of David?
segments.speaker: All the multitudes were amazed, and

segments.text: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said,
This man does not cast out demons except by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.
segments.speaker: All the multitudes were amazed, and

segments.text: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them,
Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not st and.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom st and?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come upon you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or how can one enter into the house of the strong man and plunder his goods, unless he first bind the strong man? Then he will plunder his house.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “He who is not with me is against me, and he who doesn’t gather with me, scatters.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven men.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, either in this age, or in that which is to come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree corrupt and its fruit corrupt; for the tree is known by its fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You offspring of vipers, how can you, being evil, speak good things? For out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaks.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The good man out of his good treasure[^40] brings out good things, and the evil man out of his evil treasure brings out evil things.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you that every idle word that men speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then certain of the scribes and Pharisees answered,
Teacher, we want to see a sign from you.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: But he answered them,
An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, but no sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the huge fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: The men of Nineveh will st and up in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, someone greater than Jonah is here.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, someone greater than Solomon is here.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: “When an unclean spirit has gone out of a man, he passes through waterless places seeking rest, and doesn’t find it.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: Then he says, ‘I will return into my house from which I came;’ and when he has come back, he finds it empty, swept, and put in order.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: Then he goes and takes with himself seven other spirits more evil than he is, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first. Even so will it be also to this evil generation.
segments.speaker: certain of the scribes and Pharisees

segments.text: While he was yet speaking to the multitudes, behold, his mother and his brothers stood outside, seeking to speak to him.
One said to him,
Behold, your mother and your brothers st and outside, seeking to speak to you.
segments.speaker: One

segments.text: But he answered him who spoke to him,
Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?
segments.speaker: One

segments.text: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples, and said,
Behold, my mother and my brothers!
segments.speaker: One

segments.text: For whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.
segments.speaker: One
